tool_args.label: Five risks to financial plan
tool_args.raw_text: Mr. Willens concluded by outlining five risks to the financial plan: 1. Continued paid ridership recovery... 2. Dedicated tax receipts... 3. Casino revenue timing... 4. Operating efficiencies... 5. Capital program remains funded – if the New Jersey litigation delays congestion pricing...

payload.claim_text: MTA faces five risks to the financial plan: 1) paid ridership recovery lower than projected 80% pre-pandemic could cost $325 per year for 5% lower recovery; 2) slower economy/softer real estate market could cost $250-$750M per year; 3) casino revenue timing could be slower, assumed at $500 per year in 2026 and 2027; 4) less than full operating efficiency implementation could cost $400-$500M per year; 5) NJ litigation delaying congestion pricing assumes $100-$200M per year in higher debt service costs
payload.subject: MTA Financial Plan Risks
